notes: Anonymous AutoCAD dimension block (*D355): vertical linear dimension of 10400 with DIMDOT terminators on DIM/DEFPOINTS; annotation cache with no wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: Block name *D355 follows AutoCAD's anonymous-dimension naming pattern (*D<n>). Its 3 DIM-layer LINEs form a dimension frame -- two horizontal extension lines (8D88, 8D89) and one vertical dimension line (8D8A) -- terminated by two dot (DIMDOT) INSERTs (8D8B, 8D8C), with a single MTEXT (8D8D) reading '10400', equal to the gap between the two extension lines. Three POINTs sit on DEFPOINTS, the layer AutoCAD reserves for dimension definition points.
rationale.rule: An anonymous *D<n> block whose lines sit on the DIM layer with definition points on DEFPOINTS, dot/tick terminator INSERTs and one value-bearing MTEXT is a cached dimension; its lines are extension and dimension lines, so they do not represent walls regardless of their length.
rationale.counterfactual: These parallel lines are 10400 units apart, far beyond any single wall thickness, and carry dimension terminators and a value MTEXT; the judgment would only flip toward a wall if the lines moved to an architectural layer and shed those dimension markers.
rationale.uncertainty: This projection does not expose the interior geometry of the terminator sub-blocks or which model edge the dimension measures, and layer names are only labels, so a single mis-layered line cannot be excluded with total certainty; the coincident DEFPOINTS points and value-bearing MTEXT nonetheless make a wall reading very unlikely.
